title: Open Org Idea
description: A published idea — a structured seed of intent, linked to evidence and connectable to ideas from other organisations.

x-minimum-viable-idea.description: The schema requires technical metadata (schema_version, id, published) which the agent generates. The human-meaningful minimum viable idea adds five things: a title and summary so it's readable, at least one theme so it's matchable, a place so it's locatable to local funders, and a status so it's matchable to readiness. An idea without a place can't be discovered by local funders; an idea without a status can't be matched to whether you're scoping or seeking funding. These two fields are what turn a published thought into a connection point.

id.description: Unique identifier for this idea within the organisation

org.description: The organisation publishing this idea. Required so ideas can travel through federated channels without losing their binding to a publisher.

id.description: DID or org-id.guide identifier of the publishing organisation, e.g. 'GB-CHC-1234567'

name.description: Display name of the publishing organisation

published.description: When this idea was first published

updated.description: When this idea was last modified

status.description: Lifecycle stage. 'seed' = early thinking. 'developing' = being shaped. 'ready' = could accept funding. 'funded' = resourced. 'archived' = no longer active.

summary.description: Plain language description of the idea. What, where, who for, roughly how.

narrative.description: Longer form description — inline markdown or reference to a file

themes.description: Thematic tags from the Open Org vocabulary. Shared vocabulary with the profile's mission.themes.

beneficiaries.description: Who this idea would primarily serve

indicative_cost.description: Rough costing. Not a budget — a signal of scale.

period.description: Duration the cost covers, e.g. '2 years', '12 months'

evidence_base.description: Links to evidence items from the organisation's profile that support this idea.

evidence_id.description: ID of an evidence item from the org-profile

relevance.description: Why this evidence is relevant to this idea

connections.description: Links to ideas from other organisations. The connective tissue of the network.

collaborators.description: Organisations already involved in shaping or planning to deliver this idea.

org_id.description: DID or org-id.guide identifier of the collaborating organisation

confirmed.description: Whether this collaboration is confirmed or aspirational

versions.description: Change log showing how the idea has evolved. Funders see trajectory, not just a snapshot.

access_level.description: Visibility. Most ideas should be public — that's the point.

idea_connection.description: A link between this idea and an idea published by another organisation.

org_id.description: DID or org-id.guide identifier of the other organisation

relationship.description: How this idea relates to the other. 'complementary' = different work that strengthens both. 'builds_on' = extends or depends on the other. 'alternative_approach' = different method for similar goals. 'prerequisite' = this idea needs the other to happen first. 'downstream' = this idea creates conditions for the other.

mutual.description: Whether the other organisation has confirmed this connection
